title: P2 TUI + DX: Items #17, #19, #20, #13
goal: Implement scrolling (#17), throughput stats (#19), finished-ago (#20), and backlog support (#13). TUI items in one worktree to avoid merge conflicts.

decisions.title: TUI items share one worktree
decisions.rationale: #17 (scrolling), #19 (throughput), #20 (finished-ago) all modify workflow_tui_render.py. Separate branches would conflict on merge-back. One impl agent handles all three sequentially in one worktree.

decisions.title: Backlog support gets its own worktree
decisions.rationale: #13 touches workflow_state.py, workflow_ops.py, workflow_apply.py — completely disjoint from TUI work. No file overlap with TUI lane.

decisions.title: Review per worktree, not per task
decisions.rationale: Since TUI items share a worktree, one review pass covers all three. Semantic reviewer checks correctness, standards reviewer checks quality.

phases.name: impl-phase
phases.title: Implementation
phases.goal: Implement all features in parallel worktree lanes.

jobs.name: impl-tui
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc worker. Do not run ccc or spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Implement THREE features in sequence:

1. #17: TUI pane scrolling and run-list bounds.
   - Right-hand detail pane should be scrollable with mouse wheel.
   - Left-hand run-list: fix windowing for variable-height rows (2-3 visual lines per row).
   - Add regression tests for variable-height run rows and mouse-wheel/detail-pane scrolling.

2. #19: Live throughput stats and smoothed counters.
   - All live stats boxes should show estimated tokens-per-second when token telemetry is available.
   - Monotonic numeric values (token totals, tokens/sec) should use smooth odometer-like display that increases at estimated rate while live.
   - Once completed, show exact recorded value.
   - Do NOT apply smoothing to times or dates.

3. #20: Finished-ago field for completed runs and agents.
   - Agent and run detail views should show 'finished ago' / completed-age field once status is terminal, alongside duration.
   - Live runs/agents keep showing elapsed/live duration.
   - Completed ones should show both how long they ran and how recently they finished.

Code standards: no file over 2000 LOC, no duplication, meaningful names.
After implementing all three, run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow
Commit each feature separately with messages:
  feat(workflow): add TUI pane scrolling and run-list bounds (#17)
  feat(workflow): add live throughput stats and smoothed counters (#19)
  feat(workflow): add finished-ago field for completed runs/agents (#20)

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|blocked), summary (string), files_changed (array), tests_passed (bool), commits (array).

jobs.name: impl-13
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc worker. Do not run ccc or spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Implement #13: First-class dogfood/backlog support.

What to build:
- Add a command or convention that creates a durable backlog artifact, registers it, and appends discoveries there instead of relying on bounded event history.
- The backlog should be a workflow artifact (file on disk) that survives event rollover.
- Add a `workflow backlog <run-id> --append '<finding>'` command to workflow_ops.py.
- The backlog file should be registered as an artifact in run state.
- Add tests in tests/test_workflow.py.

Code standards: no file over 2000 LOC, no duplication, meaningful names.
After implementing, run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow
Commit with message: feat(workflow): add first-class dogfood/backlog support (#13)

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|blocked), summary (string), files_changed (array), tests_passed (bool), commit (string or null).

phases.name: review-phase
phases.title: Review
phases.goal: Semantic and standards review for each worktree.

jobs.name: review-tui-a
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @glm51 review worker. Do not run ccc. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix for THREE TUI features just committed in this worktree:
- #17: Scrolling — does mouse wheel work? Variable-height rows handled? Selection stays visible?
- #19: Throughput — is tokens/sec correct? Odometer smoothing works for live vs completed?
- #20: Finished-ago — shown for terminal only? Alongside duration?

No behavioral regression in existing tests.
Fix and commit until PASS. Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow
Return strict JSON: status, findings, tests_passed, commits, notes.

jobs.name: review-tui-b
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @cx-reviewer review worker. Do not run ccc. Work in the current cwd.

Standards review for TUI features. Previous semantic review already applied.
- Code standards (LOC, duplication, naming)
- Test quality, snapshot freshness
- Re-export completeness
- Previous reviewer's fixes

Fix and commit until PASS. Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow
Return strict JSON: status, findings, tests_passed, commits, notes.

jobs.name: review-13a
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @glm51 review worker. Do not run ccc. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix for #13 (backlog support) just committed in this worktree.
- Does backlog append correctly?
- Is the artifact registered and searchable?
- Does it survive event rollover?
- Does the CLI command work?

Fix and commit until PASS. Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow
Return strict JSON: status, findings, tests_passed, commits, notes.
